title: Dictionary validator (provenance-100%, code integrity) + bounded-sample check
context: Provenance must be 100% and controlled-tier data must never slip through. This validator enforces sourceRef coverage and code/value integrity and rejects controlled accessTier via a committed fixture. Binding guardrail: open-access / aggregate / de-identified data only; controlled-access and identifiable data are EXCLUDED at gate-002; provenance on every assertion; the deliverable is documentation about the data, never the data itself.
objective: Implement the dictionary validator that enforces 100% provenance, code/value integrity, controlled-tier rejection, and an optional bounded-sample schema check.
acceptanceCriteria: Fails the build if any assertion lacks a sourceRef (provenance = 100% enforced).
Checks code/value integrity (no orphan codes, valid enums) and rejects accessTier: controlled via a committed fixture.
Optional bounded-sample schema check honors the inspection access protocol; no real data committed. Code MIT; CI green; no credentials embedded.
output: PR to the repo adding the validator plus the controlled-tier-rejection and provenance fixtures.
